text: Science reveals how anchoring bias controls your choices more than you think. Anchoring bias happens when we rely too heavily on the first piece of information we receive. Imagine seeing a $1,000 TV, then a $500 one. That $500 looks like a steal, right? Studies show people are more likely to buy when they're anchored to higher prices. Being aware of this bias can help you make smarter choices, not just in shopping, but in life. Want to learn more about your mind? Hit that follow button!
